steps.title: Spot the defining outcome
steps.content: The delta is "shaped and reshaped over time" as it eventually forms "new portions of land" from sediments carried by the river. The blank describes what these sediments do.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank must mean "build up" — sediments must gather and pile up where the river meets the sea to form new land. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — dissolve: break apart and disappear — sediments that dissolve cannot form land; this directly contradicts the outcome. ✗
  • Choice B — accelerate: speed up — this describes the pace of movement, not what the sediments do when they reach the sea to create land. ✗
  • Choice C — thrive: grow and flourish — this applies to living organisms, not sediment deposits forming land. ✗
  • Choice D — accumulate: gather and build up over time — sediments accumulating where the river meets the sea is exactly how new portions of land form. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
